source_command: manual closeout update for cost trend recency, sealed dry-run, status v2 preview, regression dry-run, and CI cost evidence
summary: Implemented the 2026-05-09 two-review actual cross-check plan and closed the release finality follow-ups: fixed-point closeout convergence is schema-backed, finality digest binding is separated into its own attestation, strict external report manifest and source-package clean-extract evidence are typed release inputs, release smoke archive boundedness is projected into closeout/dashboard evidence, standalone fixed-point runs now automatically bootstrap stale artifact-freshness schema debt after fixed-point schema migrations, finalizer duration evidence is summarized for dashboard cost review, writer cost thresholds and rolling trend evidence are preserved, dashboard cost evidence now explicitly separates current and sampled fixed-point digests, sealed closeout has both canonical and candidate-only dry-run rehearsal paths, batch status has a v2 preview migration surface, focused finality/freshness regressions are bundled into a non-mutating integration target, and CI uploads fixed-point cost evidence.

observations.problem: The closeout chain still requires careful manual ordering because artifact freshness, generated artifact index, release closeout, dashboard, batch manifest, and self-check reports can change each other's input digests.
observations.why_it_matters: Operators can reach a schema-clean state, but source_tree_coherence_attention and downstream digest mismatch evidence still require human interpretation instead of a deterministic fixed-point closeout result.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a bounded fixed-point finalizer that reruns the closeout report set until tracked digests converge or emits a typed non-convergence explanation with the remaining cycle edges.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-09: added policy-backed release-closeout-fixed-point convergence, iteration selected_targets, command duration_ms, changed-downstream reruns, fixed-point promotion, and finality attestation binding. Verified make release-evidence-closeout completes with tmp-json-clean and release-closeout-finality-verify.

observations.problem: The strict external report manifest can be generated with the current release ZIP, but it is not yet a first-class check target in the default validation or release closeout flow.
observations.why_it_matters: A future external review could be added or renamed without forcing the manifest to refresh its digest, path alias, normalization, and ZIP-basis evidence before release review.
observations.automation_candidate: Add an external-report-reference-manifest-check target that compares a regenerated strict candidate against the checked-in manifest whenever external-reports are present.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-09: added external-report-reference-manifest-release-check, wired advisory non-sealed release evidence into closeout authority, kept strict mode for sealed release flows, added CI/static workflow coverage, and bound the external report manifest digest into batch/finality inputs.

observations.surface: make release-source-package-check; ops/policies/source-package-test-deselections.json; release closeout authority reports
observations.problem: The source package clean-extract test profile exists, but its pass/fail result is not yet projected as a typed release authority input beside full-suite and report-contract evidence.
observations.why_it_matters: Source ZIP portability can regress independently from the full workspace tests, and operators should not have to infer source-package health from an ad hoc command run.
observations.automation_candidate: Promote release-source-package-check into a schema-backed summary artifact and bind its digest into release-closeout-summary, release-evidence-dashboard, and release-closeout-batch-manifest.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-09: added source-package-clean-extract report/schema/script, promoted release-source-package-check output as canonical release evidence, recorded ZIP digest, sanitized extract root, ruff/mypy/source-package pytest status, deselection budget, and reproducibility status, and wired it into closeout summary/batch authority.

observations.problem: Release smoke command records include per-command timeouts, but the release dashboard does not summarize boundedness or profile-level budget health as a separate operator-facing signal.
observations.why_it_matters: A release smoke pass can still become operationally expensive or unclear when profile scope changes, especially when full-vault corpora make smoke duration sensitive to corpus size.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a release_smoke_boundedness input summary with profile, command count, timeout budget, observed duration, and any budget attention state.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-09: release-closeout-summary now exposes release_smoke_boundedness_gate, release-evidence-dashboard exposes budget_signals.release_smoke_boundedness, and budget failure is promoted to a clean release blocker.

observations.problem: When the fixed-point report schema itself changes, the first standalone fixed-point run can refresh artifact-freshness against the previous fixed-point report before promoting the new one, requiring a second pass or a prior artifact-freshness refresh to make the canonical freshness report describe the newly promoted fixed-point schema.
observations.why_it_matters: The tracked writer digest map can still converge, but operators may see a transient artifact-freshness blocker after a schema migration unless they run the full closeout chain or repeat the standalone fixed-point target.
observations.automation_candidate: Teach the fixed-point finalizer to detect post-promote artifact-freshness schema debt and automatically perform a bounded bootstrap refresh, or split artifact-freshness into pre-finality and post-finality scopes so the self-report migration case is explicit.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-09: release-closeout-fixed-point now runs a post-promote bootstrap mode that detects canonical artifact-freshness schema_validation_failed debt for ops/reports/release-closeout-fixed-point.json, refreshes artifact-freshness, rebuilds/promotes fixed-point through a bounded pass, and fails if the debt persists. Added regression tests for both bootstrap and no-op paths.

observations.problem: The fixed-point finalizer recorded command duration_ms per iteration, but operators had no writer-level cost summary or dashboard signal proving that the DAG skip policy reduced expensive prerequisite reruns.
observations.why_it_matters: A bounded fixed-point loop can be correct but still become operationally costly; release operators need evidence that generated-artifact-index prerequisites are not rerun after the first iteration unless policy changes require it.
observations.automation_candidate: Aggregate fixed-point command duration_ms into writer rolling cost evidence and project expensive_prerequisites_once skip effectiveness into the release evidence dashboard budget signals.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-09: release-closeout-fixed-point now emits duration_summary with writer_costs and expensive_prerequisites_once skip evidence; release-evidence-dashboard exposes budget_signals.fixed_point_finalizer_cost with writer costs and post-first expensive prerequisite run counts. Added focused regression coverage and regenerated canonical closeout/finality reports.

observations.problem: Writer duration evidence was visible only for the current fixed-point report, so a cost surge could be missed and operators could not see which fixed-point digest a dashboard cost signal sampled.
observations.why_it_matters: DAG skip optimization needs durable evidence, not just one-run timing. A release dashboard should make cost attention actionable by naming the sampled fixed-point digest and rolling trend basis.
observations.automation_candidate: Persist a schema-backed rolling fixed-point cost trend artifact, apply policy thresholds to writer costs, and bind the sampled fixed-point digest and trend digest into dashboard cost evidence.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-09: added release-closeout-fixed-point-cost-trend report/schema/Make target, policy-backed writer threshold attention, retained-run trend samples, and dashboard evidence_basis with current_fixed_point_report_digest, sampled_fixed_point_report_digest, basis_relation_to_current_fixed_point, and rolling trend digest. Added focused tests for trend persistence, threshold breach attention, dashboard projection, and stale sample relation labeling.

observations.problem: The sealed release lane could materialize a distribution ZIP and strict external manifest, but the release tier did not have one focused assertion that the distribution ZIP, batch sealed status, and strict external manifest were bound to the same artifact.
observations.why_it_matters: A sealed release rehearsal should fail fast if the batch manifest claims sealed_clean_pass without strict external review binding or if strict manifest evidence points at a different ZIP.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a sealed closeout check target that validates distribution ZIP digest/entry count, batch sealed_release_status, release authority, distribution binding, and strict external manifest basis.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-09: release-evidence-closeout-sealed now runs release-evidence-closeout-sealed-check, which asserts sealed_clean_pass, strict_review_release external manifest binding, current distribution ZIP digest/entry-count agreement, and batch distribution materialization. Added candidate manifest path support and release-evidence-closeout-sealed-dry-run, which builds tmp ZIP/strict external manifest/batch manifest/check without canonical promotion. The current dry-run reaches strict ZIP binding but reports conditional authority as a typed rehearsal failure.

observations.problem: Batch manifest top-level status remains a legacy strict clean plus sealed claim while release_authority_status carries semantic release authority and sealed_release_status carries artifact distribution finality.
observations.why_it_matters: Operators and downstream checks can misread top-level status as the release authority decision even when non-sealed closeout intentionally reports conditional_pass authority.
observations.automation_candidate: Document the three status roles in schema/report output and prepare a later migration toward explicit sealed_status naming.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-09: batch manifest now emits status_semantics and status_v2_preview, schema descriptions clarify top-level status versus release_authority_status, and README documents the legacy naming boundary plus the v2 preview path toward explicit release authority and sealed status axes.

observations.problem: Finality mismatch, batch finality ownership, source package clean-extract failure, and release smoke archive budget failure regressions were covered in separate tests but not available as a focused release-closeout regression suite.
observations.why_it_matters: Release closeout safety depends on these edge cases staying green together; operators need a narrow target for quick validation without running the full suite.
observations.automation_candidate: Bundle the focused regression nodeids into a Make target and add a CI tier that runs exactly those release-closeout regressions.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-09: added test-release-closeout-regression-pack, release-closeout-regression-dry-run, and a release-closeout-regression CI matrix lane covering finality digest drift, batch digest ownership, source package clean-extract failure, release smoke archive budget failure, tmp artifact-freshness check, and finality verify without canonical mutation.

observations.problem: Fixed-point cost evidence could be inspected locally, but CI did not preserve a per-run cost trend artifact alongside the sampled fixed-point and dashboard reports.
observations.why_it_matters: When a release-closeout-regression CI lane catches a duration regression, the reviewer should have the exact cost evidence JSON rather than relying on console logs.
observations.automation_candidate: Generate a tmp fixed-point cost trend artifact in the release-closeout-regression tier and upload it with the fixed-point, cost trend, and dashboard reports.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-09: added release-closeout-cost-evidence-ci-artifact and a GitHub Actions upload-artifact step for tmp/release-closeout-fixed-point-cost-trend-ci.json plus canonical fixed-point, cost-trend, and dashboard reports.
